intake.finding: Classified as a new feature under Blink>PerformanceAPIs and incubated in WICG (proposal #264 opened 2026-02-04 by yoavweiss). ChromeStatus entry is complete with motivation, explainer link, bug, and owners (yoavweiss + barrypollard). Mode/target layer (a performance measurement API) is appropriate. No public target-segment map, usage inventory, or highest-risk-assumption list in the record.
intake.limitations: Intake artifacts (segment map, risk-assumption inventory) are not present in the public ChromeStatus record.

incubation.finding: A real, well-articulated developer problem: developers using speculative loading cannot tell whether preloads/prefetches/prerenders were used, so cannot weigh performance gains against wasted bandwidth and server load. Explainer documents goals, non-goals, and three considered alternatives (PerformanceObserver, dismissal-event exposure, Reporting API) with reasons. Parent Speculation Rules API has broad adoption, validating latent demand. However the incubation evidence ledger is thin and owner-driven: WICG repo has only 5 stars, 4 open issues (2 from external developers), and no published surveys, interviews, or segment research.
incubation.limitations: No representative, methods-documented developer research (survey/interview) was found in public sources.

prototype.finding: An implementation exists and powers the active OT (chromium trial name SpeculationMeasurement; UseCounter bucket 5897 / kSpeculationMeasurement). API shape is documented with an example, and Intent to Prototype is recorded (ChromeStatus stage 130). However doc_links and sample_links are empty, and no public task-based samples, friction logs, or explainer-vs-implementation divergence records exist.
prototype.limitations: No public friction-log, integration-spike, or framework/server compatibility evidence for the prototype.

developerTrials.finding: No explicit behind-a-flag developer trial is recorded; the feature moved from WICG incubation directly to an Origin Trial. Nonetheless the WICG repo actively solicits developer feedback (issue #8, opened 2026-03-11) and received substantive external feedback (#14 CDN/preconnect pain from krzksz; #15 ecommerce lead-time from ErikWitt).
developerTrials.limitations: No structured cohort, recruitment, task-completion tracking, or methodology; feedback is ad-hoc GitHub issues rather than a deliberate developer trial.

wideReview.finding: The weakest dimension at OT registration. tag_review_status, security_review_status, and privacy_review_status are all 'Pending' in ChromeStatus; Firefox and Safari are both 'No signal' and webdev 'No signals'; standards maturity is 'Unknown' with no spec_link. For an OT that exposes per-user navigation and preload-usage data this is a material privacy and interoperability risk. The parent Speculation Rules API has Mozilla (#620) and WebKit (#54) positions and is an Interop 2025 focus-area proposal, but this measurement API has no other-engine engagement.
wideReview.limitations: Only public ChromeStatus review fields are visible; private review activity cannot be confirmed or ruled out. 'No signal' must not be read as neutral or positive.

experiment.finding: OT is properly configured and active: Intent to Experiment sent 2026-05-19, received 'LGTM to experiment M150-M155' (desktop); third-party OT support enabled; OT id 2748884622556266497; documentation and feedback URLs set; owner barrypollard, requester yoavweiss; registered on the Chrome Origin Trials listing. However the public experiment protocol (hypotheses, metrics, denominators, decision thresholds, privacy handling) is not stated, and the OT description uses a different API name ('performance.speculations') than the explainer ('performance.getSpeculations()'). No interim findings are published yet (OT in early weeks, Chrome 150 ~mid-2026).
experiment.limitations: Experiment design/protocol and any interim readouts are not public; OT is too recent for results.

prepareToShip.finding: Not yet entered. The feature is in Origin Trial (ChromeStatus status 'Proposed', is_released false); default availability is not intended or reached.
prepareToShip.limitations: Applies only once an Intent to Ship is pursued; no prepare-to-ship work exists yet.

release.finding: No default-availability or beta release event exists for this feature; the only launch event is the Origin Trial at milestone 150. Feature is not shipped by default.
release.limitations: Release communication phase does not apply until default shipment.

adoption.finding: The OT only just began (Chrome 150, mid-2026); no public usage, UseCounter telemetry, or production case study of the measurement API (getSpeculations) exists. Broad adoption of the parent Speculation Rules API (WordPress core, Ray-Ban, Shopify case studies) validates latent demand but is not adoption of THIS measurement surface.
adoption.limitations: No OT participant count, activation telemetry, or production deployment of the measurement API is public.

support.finding: Feedback is routed to a public GitHub repo with owner engagement (yoavweiss/barrypollard) and four logged issues, including the owner opening design-gap issues (#11). But there is no published FAQ, troubleshooting pack, or support-health dashboard, and issue volume is small.
support.limitations: Support load is not yet representative; no recurrence/severity taxonomy or resolution-time data.

deprecation.finding: New feature in WICG incubation/OT; nothing to deprecate or remove.
deprecation.limitations: Deprecation/removal does not apply to a newly introduced API.

developerValue.finding: The problem is real and externally validated: production ecommerce (#15) and CDN-fronted (#14) developers need to measure speculation effectiveness and wasted bandwidth, and the parent feature is widely deployed. The API covers preloads plus speculation-rule navigational prefetches/prerenders and exposes actionable metadata (as, crossorigin, tags, eagerness, earlyhint). But material developer needs are uncovered at OT start: a start-time timestamp to measure lead time (#15), preconnect/dns-prefetch measurement (#14), and the cost of prerender_until_script assets (#11, owner-opened and unresolved).
developerValue.limitations: No measured developer outcome data yet; feature is OT-stage.

endUserImpact.finding: Indirect end-user benefit only: better-tuned speculation could speed navigations and reduce wasted bandwidth/server load. No accessibility, privacy, or end-user-control evidence and no measured end-user impact at OT stage.
endUserImpact.limitations: No end-user outcome evidence available for a measurement/observability API in early OT.

adoption.finding: No public adoption or usage data for getSpeculations; OT is in its early weeks (Chrome 150, mid-2026).
adoption.limitations: Usage/adoption of this specific API is unknown; cannot be inferred from parent-feature adoption.

interoperability.finding: Chrome-only OT. Firefox and Safari both 'No signal'; no Mozilla or WebKit position on this measurement API was found; tag review pending; WICG-only incubation. This creates a concrete risk of a Chrome-only observability surface even though the parent Speculation Rules API already has cross-engine positions and is an Interop 2025 focus area.
interoperability.limitations: Other-engine positions could emerge later; the current public state is no signal.

implementationQuality.finding: An implementation exists and runs in OT (registered chromium trial, UseCounter bucket 5897). No public WPT results, regression data, or compatibility evidence for this API were found.
implementationQuality.limitations: No public test/quality evidence; not scoreable.

evidenceAndCommunication.finding: ChromeStatus motivation and summary are consistent with the explainer, which documents goals/non-goals/alternatives well, and an LGTM to experiment was obtained. But three API names coexist (getSpeculations() in the ChromeStatus summary and explainer; 'performance.speculations' in the OT registration description; trial name SpeculationMeasurement); tag/security/privacy reviews are all 'Pending' at OT registration; and doc_links and sample_links are empty.
evidenceAndCommunication.limitations: No public-facing reference docs, samples, or FAQ exist yet for the measurement API.

support.finding: A public GitHub feedback channel with active owner response and genuine external developer engagement exists. But volume is low, there is no published FAQ/troubleshooting, and three of the four open issues are unresolved feature/coverage gaps rather than support resolutions.
support.limitations: Too early for support load to be representative; no resolution-time or recurrence data.

overall.finding: A well-motivated, properly-configured Origin Trial for a real developer problem, with genuine external developer engagement. However it is early-stage with thin independent research, no other-engine signal, pending formal reviews, known API coverage gaps, and an API-naming inconsistency. Outcomes are not yet measurable.
overall.limitations: Overall outcome cannot be judged beyond OT setup quality until usage, interop, and field results exist.

supports: Debuggability tooling (Speculative loads panel) exists for the parent Speculation Rules API
sources.limitations: Search snippet only; parent-feature tooling, not this measurement API.

supports: Parent-feature production outcomes: Ray-Ban 43% mobile LCP improvement via prerender; Shopify prefetch rollout
sources.limitations: Search snippet only; parent-feature adoption used as problem-validation proxy, not adoption of getSpeculations.

supports: Independent testing of parent Speculation Rules API effect on page speed (prerender ~47% reduction in one test)
sources.limitations: Search snippet only; parent feature; single-test methodology.

successes: Real, externally-validated developer problem: production ecommerce (#15) and CDN/Early-Hints (#14) developers need to measure speculation effectiveness and wasted bandwidth.

Clean explainer with explicit goals, non-goals, and three considered alternatives with reasons; the API shape evolved from a pagehide attribute to performance.getSpeculations() in response to incubation feedback.
Properly configured Origin Trial with third-party support, documentation and feedback URLs, and a documented 'LGTM to experiment M150-M155'.
Active, owner-engaged public feedback channel producing substantive external feature requests (#14, #15).
failures: No Mozilla or WebKit signal on the measurement API, and tag/security/privacy reviews all 'Pending' at OT registration — interoperability and privacy-review gaps for an OT exposing per-user navigation and preload-usage data.

API-naming inconsistency: explainer/ChromeStatus summary say performance.getSpeculations(), the OT registration description says 'performance.speculations', and the trial name is SpeculationMeasurement.
Material developer needs uncovered at OT start: lead-time/start-time (#15), preconnect/dns-prefetch measurement (#14), and prerender_until_script asset cost (#11, owner-opened and unresolved).
Empty doc_links/sample_links and no public experiment protocol (hypotheses, metrics, denominators, decision thresholds).
counterfactuals: A pre-OT friction log run with production speculation users (ecommerce and CDN/Early-Hints deployments) would likely have surfaced the lead-time (#15), preconnect (#14), and prerender_until_script asset-cost (#11) gaps before field exposure, narrowing the OT's redesign surface.

Enforcing one canonical API surface name across the ChromeStatus summary, the OT registration, the explainer, and the use counter before registration would have reduced fragmented feedback and analytics integration.
A privacy review preceding OT registration would have given participants and reviewers a clearer data-minimization story for an API that exposes per-user navigation/preload usage.
Mozilla/WebKit outreach during WICG incubation (the parent feature already has their positions) would have lowered the risk of this becoming a Chrome-only observability API.
skillImprovements: Add a phase-05 (experiment) transition-packet rule requiring a single canonical API surface name across the ChromeStatus summary, OT display name/description, explainer, and UseCounter; treat any discrepancy as a blocker before OT registration.

Add a pre-OT friction-log requirement for observability/measurement APIs that exercises production speculation users across ecommerce and CDN/Early-Hints deployments to surface coverage gaps before field exposure.

Require that any new performance/observability API exposing per-user navigation/usage data carry a documented privacy-review status (and ideally completion) before OT registration, rather than 'Pending'.

Strengthen phase-04 (wide review) to require documented Mozilla/WebKit outreach for new WICG performance/observability APIs and to render 'No signal' explicitly as an OT risk in the transition packet, never as neutral/positive.
Add a regression eval that verifies an OT states its hypotheses, metrics, denominators, and decision thresholds publicly before it opens.

Mozilla/WebKit standards position on the measurement API specifically (none found; consistent with ChromeStatus 'No signal').
tag/security/privacy review outcomes (all 'Pending' in ChromeStatus at retrieval).
Public experiment protocol and interim Origin Trial findings (not published).
Framework/library integration of getSpeculations (none found; OT-stage).
